votes.reason: The Python code correctly computes the sum and product of the list elements, matching the fold_left operations in the Coq specification.
The Python code correctly computes the sum and product of the list elements, matching the fold_left operations in the Coq specification.
The Python code correctly computes the sum and product of the list elements, matching the fold_left operations in the Coq specification.

The Python code computes sum using sum(l) which is equivalent to fold_left Z.add l 0, and computes product by iterating and multiplying which is equivalent to fold_left Z.mul l 1. Both return the same concrete results for any list of integers.

The Python code computes sum using sum(l) which is equivalent to fold_left Z.add l 0, and product using a loop starting from 1 which is equivalent to fold_left Z.mul l 1. Both return the same concrete results for any list of integers.
Both compute sum with start 0 and product with start 1 over integer lists (including empty), matching Coq’s fold_left with Z.add and Z.mul.
Both compute sum with initial 0 and product with initial 1 over integer lists; Python ints match Coq Z behavior, including empty list cases.
